meanings.definition: make physical contact with, come in contact with
examples: Touch the stone for good luck
She never touched her husband
Helen Keller felt the physical world by touching people and objects around her
A stirring movie
I was touched by your kind letter of sympathy
There were lots of questions referring to her talk
My remark pertained to your earlier comments

meanings.definition: be in direct physical contact with; make contact
examples: The two buildings touch
Their hands touched
The wire must not contact the metal cover
The surfaces contact at this point
Will the new rules affect me?

meanings.definition: deal with; usually used with a form of negation
examples: I wouldn't touch her with a ten-foot pole
The local Mafia won't touch gambling

meanings.definition: cause to be in brief contact with
examples: He touched his toes to the horse's flanks
The sunlight reached the wall
Can he reach?
The chair must not touch the wall

meanings.definition: be equal to in quality or ability
examples: Nothing can rival cotton for durability
Your performance doesn't even touch that of your colleagues
Her persistence and ambition only matches that of her parents
Don't touch my CDs!

meanings.definition: make a more or less disguised reference to
examples: He alluded to the problem but did not mention it
He could not touch the meaning of the poem
She didn't touch her food all night
her greying hair was tinged blond
the leaves were tinged red in November

meanings.definition: being excited or provoked to the expression of an emotion
examples: too moved to speak
very touched by the stranger's kindness
